jokes.text: Charlie: 'there's asbestos in the ducts, which... Good for breathing, so I'm tired.'
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining why he's tired from electrical work
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions asbestos is 'good for breathing' with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - confidently wrong about deadly materials. High character integration and quotable absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'there's asbestos in the ducts, which... Good for breathing, so I'm tired.'

jokes.text: Dee: 'Oh, I haven't had a chance to shower for a few days. I've been gaming like a loon.'
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Dee is sick
jokes.punchline: She casually admits to days of poor hygiene due to gaming addiction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how gaming has consumed Dee's basic self-care

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, I haven't had a chance to shower for a few days. I've been gaming like a loon.

jokes.text: Charlie getting repeatedly shocked while delivering his defiant speech about having potential
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to assert his worth and potential
jokes.punchline: Constantly interrupted by electrical shocks from the jury-rigged setup
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy undermining Charlie's rare moment of self-assertion

dialogue.line: Charlie getting repeatedly shocked while delivering his defiant speech about having potential

jokes.text: You treat me like a dumb-dumb and a grunt, and I have potential, you know? I can go places, I can do things. Who knows? I might even rule the world one day.
jokes.setup: Charlie is frustrated with how he's treated
jokes.punchline: His declaration of world-ruling potential while covered in dirt and getting shocked
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode theme. Charlie's grandiose delusions contrasted with his pathetic reality. Title reference.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You treat me like a dumb-dumb and a grunt, and I have potential, you know? I can go places, I can do things. Who knows? I might even rule the world one day.

jokes.text: Mac: 'Rule the world, huh? Yeah, if that happens, I'll blow myself.'
jokes.setup: Charlie declares he might rule the world someday
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual, matter-of-fact response about self-fellatio
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered deadpan absurdity with excellent economy. Classic Sunny shock humor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: 'Rule the world, huh? Yeah, if that happens, I'll blow myself.'

jokes.text: ZingingCutie23. Are you pretending that you're 23 years old? It's a random number that I picked.
jokes.setup: Dee reveals her gaming username
jokes.punchline: The obviously false claim that '23' is random when she's clearly trying to seem younger
jokes.explanation: Classic middle-aged person online behavior. Dee's defensive lie is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: ZingingCutie23.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Are you pretending that you're 23 years old?

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: It's a random number that I picked.

jokes.text: Dee's avatar choices: Mac as handmaiden, Dennis as midget blacksmith, Frank as mutant half-donkey, Charlie as jester
jokes.setup: Dee shows Charlie her video game where she's recreated the gang
jokes.punchline: Each avatar is a humiliating version that reflects how Dee sees them
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - shows Dee's petty vindictiveness and how she views each gang member

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Mac as handmaiden, Dennis as midget blacksmith, Frank as mutant half-donkey, Charlie as jester

jokes.text: Charlie: 'Why am I eating out of a trough?' Dee: 'Made sense.'
jokes.setup: Charlie questioning his degrading avatar treatment
jokes.punchline: Dee's casual dismissal with zero justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's casual cruelty and lack of self-reflection

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'Why am I eating out of a trough?'

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: 'Made sense.'

jokes.text: You've been playing this game for, like, a week straight just so you feel like you lord over all of us? Is that what this is?
jokes.setup: Charlie realizes what Dee's game really represents
jokes.punchline: He accurately diagnoses her pathetic power fantasy
jokes.explanation: Charlie showing unusual insight into Dee's psychology. Rare moment of clarity.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You've been playing this game for, like, a week straight just so you feel like you lord over all of us? Is that what this is?

jokes.text: Bar patron recognizing Frank and Mac: 'You're the donkey man and the princess, right?'
jokes.setup: Gang trying to escape virtual humiliation by going to real bar
jokes.punchline: Their game avatars have leaked into real world recognition
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing virtual consequences bleeding into reality, well-structured payoff

dialogue.speaker: Bar patron
dialogue.line: You're the donkey man and the princess, right?

jokes.text: Stop getting invested in fake realities! Let's live real life!
jokes.setup: Dennis is trying to convince everyone to ignore the game
jokes.punchline: He's saying this while obsessing over virtual humiliation himself
jokes.explanation: Dennis's hypocrisy is evident - he's as invested as anyone. Sets up episode theme about reality vs. virtual.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Stop getting invested in fake realities! Let's live real life!

jokes.text: Frank making stock trades while playing the game, saying 'ZingingCutie44 is back, so I'm out'
jokes.setup: Frank appears to be doing serious business
jokes.punchline: He's actually prioritizing the video game over financial trades
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how the game has consumed everyone's priorities

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: ZingingCutie44 is back, so I'm out

jokes.text: You sucked at it, all right? All you did was spend your money on stuff you don't need, just like in real life.
jokes.setup: Dee protests Charlie's management of her game
jokes.punchline: Charlie accurately diagnoses her spending problems in both virtual and real life
jokes.explanation: Charlie showing business acumen and insight into Dee's character flaws. Good parallel structure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You sucked at it, all right? All you did was spend your money on stuff you don't need, just like in real life.

jokes.text: Charlie's elaborate explanation of energy balls made from milk, flour, and vitamins
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining what he'd do with money instead of gaming
jokes.punchline: His 'responsible' choice is bizarre survival food preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - trying to be responsible but in the most bizarre way possible

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie's elaborate explanation of energy balls made from milk, flour, and vitamins

jokes.text: Dee getting fingernails done like Flo-Jo for the game
jokes.setup: Charlie notices something different about Dee
jokes.punchline: She's gotten elaborate athletic fingernails for a video game character
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag showing how gaming is affecting real-world choices

dialogue.line: Dee getting fingernails done like Flo-Jo for the game

jokes.text: I would buy milk, I would buy flour, I'd buy vitamins, I'd boil them down to little energy balls to sustain me, but whatever.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains what he'd do with the money
jokes.punchline: His bizarre survival food plan involving 'energy balls'
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie logic - completely impractical 'practical' solution. Sets up energy balls as a running element.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I would buy milk, I would buy flour, I'd buy vitamins, I'd boil them down to little energy balls to sustain me, but whatever.

jokes.text: Ew, no. I don't want to do that 'cause we'd have to be married, and that would be awful.
jokes.setup: Charlie wants to be made king in the game
jokes.punchline: Dee's visceral rejection of virtual marriage to Charlie
jokes.explanation: Standard character dynamics - Dee's disgust at Charlie is expected but still funny.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Ew, no. I don't want to do that 'cause we'd have to be married, and that would be awful.

jokes.text: Either way.
jokes.setup: Dee clarifies they'd have sex 'in the game'
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual 'either way' suggests he's open to real sex too
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that make the situation much more uncomfortable. Classic Charlie boundary issues.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Either way.

jokes.text: Stop fighting every guy in the game. The fighting is the best part. It's the only reason I'm playing.
jokes.setup: Mac keeps losing fights in the game
jokes.punchline: He only plays to fight, despite being terrible at it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac behavior - obsessed with combat despite complete incompetence. Character integration is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Stop fighting every guy in the game. The fighting is the best part. It's the only reason I'm playing.

jokes.text: Frank describing his female avatar: 'I'm banging all the fishermen in sight for fish and I'm also being catty with all the women in the game, like I'm a Real Housewife'
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his game strategy
jokes.punchline: He's role-playing as a sexually aggressive reality TV stereotype
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Frank's complete commitment to his persona

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm banging all the fishermen in sight for fish and I'm also being catty with all the women in the game, like I'm a Real Housewife

jokes.text: Mac, you're five-foot-nine, and Frank, you're a man... an ugly, old man!
jokes.setup: Dennis is describing everyone's avatars
jokes.punchline: His casual cruelty toward Frank, treating ugliness as a given
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly. The pause before 'ugly, old man' adds emphasis.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mac, you're five-foot-nine, and Frank, you're a man... an ugly, old man!

jokes.text: Dennis's sex tape featuring elaborate hair-cutting foreplay
jokes.setup: Gang watching Dennis's private video
jokes.punchline: It's an awkward, overly elaborate seduction involving professional hair styling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - his narcissistic need to make everything about his performance

jokes.text: See, now all three of us are aroused, and there's not a woman in the room. See, this is no different than sports or video games. It's all a virtual experience.
jokes.setup: Everyone is aroused watching Dennis
jokes.punchline: Charlie's pseudo-philosophical point about virtual experiences
jokes.explanation: Charlie making an unexpectedly sophisticated point about virtual vs real experiences, tying to episode theme.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: See, now all three of us are aroused, and there's not a woman in the room. See, this is no different than sports or video games. It's all a virtual experience.

jokes.text: Frank: 'We could be in a turtle's dream in outer space.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about what reality even is
jokes.punchline: Frank's completely nonsensical but confident philosophical observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd line with perfect economy. Highly quotable and captures Frank's bizarre wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: 'We could be in a turtle's dream in outer space.'

jokes.text: All right, don't interrupt me when I'm running our tribe, okay, 'cause that's kind of a big deal.
jokes.setup: Charlie has become successful in the game
jokes.punchline: Treats virtual tribe management like serious business leadership
jokes.explanation: Charlie's newfound arrogance about virtual achievements. Power corrupts even Charlie.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: All right, don't interrupt me when I'm running our tribe, okay, 'cause that's kind of a big deal.

jokes.text: Frank as SweetPeaches69 wearing mink coat, claiming 'Guy gave it to me for going down on him'
jokes.setup: Charlie surprised by Frank's success in the game
jokes.punchline: Frank casually describes trading sexual favors for luxury items
jokes.explanation: Good shock humor that fits Frank's character while advancing the game subplot

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Guy gave it to me for going down on him

jokes.text: Frank, you are SweetPeaches69? How did you get so many followers?
jokes.setup: Dee discovers Frank's female avatar success
jokes.punchline: Frank has become very popular as a woman online
jokes.explanation: Frank's username reveals his commitment to the female persona. Sets up his success story.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Frank, you are SweetPeaches69? How did you get so many followers?

jokes.text: Frank: 'I just got sent a whole box of peaches... real ones. I'm eating like a goddamn islander.'
jokes.setup: Frank bragging about his gaming success
jokes.punchline: His luxury is a box of fruit that makes him feel like he's living primitively
jokes.explanation: Solid Frank absurdity with good character voice and economy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: 'I just got sent a whole box of peaches... real ones. I'm eating like a goddamn islander.'

jokes.text: You want mink, Dee, you want a mink coat? You want a nice, real mink coat, not a virtual one like Frank?
jokes.setup: Dee is jealous of Frank's success
jokes.punchline: Charlie offers to get her a real mink coat, showing his growing power
jokes.explanation: Charlie's transformation into someone with actual influence. The real vs virtual distinction is clever.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You want mink, Dee, you want a mink coat? You want a nice, real mink coat, not a virtual one like Frank?

jokes.text: Charlie's psychological dominance over Dee: 'Be pretty, you know, talk to me right' / 'You're not pretty when you do that'
jokes.setup: Charlie has gained power in the game
jokes.punchline: He's become a controlling, manipulative partner using classic abusive language
jokes.explanation: Excellent character reversal showing how power corrupts even Charlie. Dark but well-executed

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Be pretty, you know, talk to me right

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You're not pretty when you do that

jokes.text: Well, well, well, if it isn't the soiled fool.
jokes.setup: Mac arrives to talk to Charlie
jokes.punchline: Charlie now uses the game insults in real life
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how virtual dynamics bleed into reality. Charlie's newfound arrogance.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, well, well, if it isn't the soiled fool.

jokes.text: Mac getting injured fighting his computer
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his black eye
jokes.punchline: He literally got into a physical fight with his computer and lost
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that fits Mac's aggressive personality and gaming frustration

dialogue.line: Mac getting injured fighting his computer

jokes.text: It bashed me back a little bit. Yeah, you should win those fights, you shouldn't lose... I did win, I did win, it just got a couple of good shots in.
jokes.setup: Mac's black eye from destroying his computer
jokes.punchline: Mac insists he 'won' the fight against his computer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - losing to an inanimate object but claiming victory. Classic character behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It bashed me back a little bit. Yeah, you should win those fights, you shouldn't lose... I did win, I did win, it just got a couple of good shots in.

jokes.text: Of course I... of course, that's what I was doing. Go ahead, have as many as you want... Have a bunch of balls.
jokes.setup: Charlie demands Dee serve Mac energy balls
jokes.punchline: Dee's subservient scrambling to please Charlie
jokes.explanation: Dee's complete reversal from dominant to subservient. The energy balls continue as a power symbol.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Of course I... of course, that's what I was doing. Go ahead, have as many as you want... Have a bunch of balls.

jokes.text: You, like, totally psychologically, like, annihilated her.
jokes.setup: Mac observes Charlie's treatment of Dee
jokes.punchline: Mac's impressed admiration for psychological manipulation
jokes.explanation: Mac's appreciation for dominance tactics. Standard but effective character beat.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You, like, totally psychologically, like, annihilated her.

jokes.text: She's probably fat, right? Probably very fat, yeah. If she's got any fat friends, I'm, I'm in. Or ugly. Yeah, or ugly. If they're sick, that's fine, too.
jokes.setup: Charlie is meeting a girl from online
jokes.punchline: Mac's progressively lowered standards for women
jokes.explanation: Mac's desperation escalates through the list. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She's probably fat, right? Probably very fat, yeah. If she's got any fat friends, I'm, I'm in. Or ugly. Yeah, or ugly. If they're sick, that's fine, too.

jokes.text: British Dennis: 'I've known beggars to be kings and kings to be beggars, all because they believed it so.'
jokes.setup: Dennis having a philosophical awakening
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-profound fortune cookie wisdom delivered with British accent gravitas
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's pretentious nature but not particularly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I've known beggars to be kings and kings to be beggars, all because they believed it so.

jokes.text: Charlie's gift to Queen of Thrones: 'Just a box of spiders'
jokes.setup: Charlie embarrassed about his modest gift after receiving cheesecake
jokes.punchline: His gift is a box of live spiders
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic and excellent misdirection. The casual delivery makes it even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Just a box of spiders

jokes.text: Charlie's betrayal speech: 'You thought you could make a move on me, Queen of Thrones? Well, you were wrong.'
jokes.setup: Julie thought this was just a friendly lunch
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals it was all a strategic game attack
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the game has corrupted Charlie's humanity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You thought you could make a move on me, Queen of Thrones? Well, you were wrong.

jokes.text: Charlie: 'You stupid, fat pig.' Julie: 'I'm not fat.' Charlie: 'No, you're not fat, but you were... you were supposed to be.'
jokes.setup: Charlie insulting Julie
jokes.punchline: He admits his disappointment that she doesn't fit Mac's prediction
jokes.explanation: Good callback but the humor is fairly mean-spirited even for Sunny

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'You stupid, fat pig.'

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: 'I'm not fat.'

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'No, you're not fat, but you were... you were supposed to be.'

jokes.text: Where are your 101 Dalmatians tonight? He's better at the zings than you!
jokes.setup: Frank mocks Dee's spotted coat
jokes.punchline: Dee admits Frank is better at insults than she is
jokes.explanation: Standard insult comedy. Dee's admission of defeat is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Where are your 101 Dalmatians tonight?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: He's better at the zings than you!

jokes.text: Dee sleeping with Mac to humiliate Charlie, but actually humiliating herself
jokes.setup: Dee thought she was getting revenge on Charlie
jokes.punchline: Mac points out she humiliated herself more than anyone
jokes.explanation: Solid irony showing how the characters' schemes backfire on themselves

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dee sleeping with Mac to humiliate Charlie, but actually humiliating herself

jokes.text: I know you were gonna leave me for the Queen of Thrones, so I've slept with Mac to humiliate you.
jokes.setup: Dee wants a divorce in the game
jokes.punchline: Her logic for virtual adultery as preemptive revenge
jokes.explanation: Dee's petty revenge logic. Virtual adultery treated as serious betrayal.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: I know you were gonna leave me for the Queen of Thrones, so I've slept with Mac to humiliate you.

jokes.text: Charlie demanding shares of Paddy's Pub and making Dee do 'Charlie-work' for eternity
jokes.setup: Charlie has gained power and is making demands
jokes.punchline: His demands include creating a permanent caste system with Dee at the bottom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to show mythology. Charlie's power trip reaches its peak

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I want shares of Paddy's Pub and I want Dee to do 'Charlie-work' for eternity

jokes.text: Dennis attempting British accent: 'That was British?!' Charlie: 'Yeah, I had it so good in my head'
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to make a dramatic entrance with British accent
jokes.punchline: Everyone's horrified reaction to his terrible accent attempt
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy showing Dennis's delusions about his abilities

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That was British?!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I had it so good in my head

jokes.text: Stop... This game has gone on long enough. (normal voice): Uh, shit. Are you doing an accent? Yeah, I was doing a British acc... It was a British accent. That was British?!
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to enter dramatically with his British accent
jokes.punchline: His accent is so bad that even these people don't recognize it
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the tank hallucination. Dennis's confidence in his terrible accent is perfect character work.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Stop... This game has gone on long enough.
Uh, shit. Are you doing an accent?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was doing a British acc... It was a British accent.
That was British?!

jokes.text: The only thing bigger than a king is a god. You think you're a god? I'm the one who's thriving, Dennis.
jokes.setup: Dennis challenges Charlie's power
jokes.punchline: Both claim god-like status, with Charlie using 'thriving' again
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of competing delusions. Callbacks to both Dennis's god complex and Charlie's thriving theme.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The only thing bigger than a king is a god. You think you're a god?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm the one who's thriving, Dennis.

jokes.text: Dennis: 'So, you see, I unplugged it.' Charlie: 'You... unplugged reality?'
jokes.setup: Dennis's dramatic buildup about controlling reality
jokes.punchline: He literally just unplugged the game server
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and deflation. Perfect ending to Dennis's god complex arc

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So, you see, I unplugged it.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You... unplugged reality?

jokes.text: Everyone having same password 'paddyspub'
jokes.setup: How did Dennis delete everyone's characters?
jokes.punchline: They all use the most obvious password possible
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/punchline that explains the plot while fitting character stupidity

dialogue.line: Everyone having same password 'paddyspub'

jokes.text: Dennis's casual dismissal: 'Sometimes things just sort of... end.'
jokes.setup: Everyone devastated by losing their game progress
jokes.punchline: Dennis's completely nonchalant shrug about destroying their obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis sociopathic indifference. Good episode ending that deflates all the buildup

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Sometimes things just sort of... end.
